name: Tour of the outskirts of Alicante — Circular from Callosa de Segura
name_original: Tour por los confines de Alicante — Circular desde Callosa de Segura
region_intro: Road cycling around Callosa De Segura offers diverse terrain, characterized by the dramatic limestone peaks of the Sierra de Callosa rising from the fertile plains of the Vega Baja del Segura. This region features extensive orchards, traditional irrigation systems, and scenic routes around natural features like La Pedrera Reservoir. The landscape provides a mix of flat, paved sections and challenging ascents, catering to various road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Callosa De Segura The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the outskirts of Alicante — Circular from Callosa de Segura, a 44.3 miles (71.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route explores the broader Alicante region, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Miguel de Salinas Church – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Callosa de Segura, a moderate 54.5 miles (87.6 km) path. This route passes by the San Miguel de Salinas Church and offers views of La Pedrera Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Bigastro–Rojales Road – View of Embalse de la Pedrera loop from Callosa de Segura, a 65.3 miles (105.0 km) trail leading through the area around La Pedrera Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Callosa De Segura is defined by the Sierra de Callosa mountain range, the agricultural plains of Vega Baja del Segura, and the scenic La Pedrera Reservoir. The network offers options for both relaxed rides on paved surfaces and more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Callosa De Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Callosa De Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Callosa De Segura?
A: Road cycling in Callosa De Segura offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find extensive flat, paved sections through the fertile Vega Baja del Segura, characterized by orchards and traditional irrigation systems. However, the dramatic limestone peaks of the Sierra de Callosa also provide challenging ascents with significant elevation gain for those seeking more demanding rides.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Callosa De Segura?
A: Yes, Callosa De Segura offers routes suitable for beginners. The region has over 10 easy road cycling routes, often traversing the flat plains of the Vega Baja del Segura. These routes typically have minimal elevation changes, making them ideal for a relaxed experience. For example, the "Ciclorruta Los Almarjales" and "Ciclorruta Los Azarbes" are dedicated cycling paths that are predominantly paved and accessible.

Q: What options are there for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find plenty of challenging options in Callosa De Segura. The region features 4 difficult routes and 28 moderate routes, many of which include significant elevation gain, particularly around the Sierra de Callosa. The area is also recognized as an ideal location for winter training, with the Alto de Aitana, the highest point in Alicante province, offering a demanding climb for serious riders.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Callosa De Segura?
A: Road cycling routes in Callosa De Segura often pass by significant natural features and landmarks. You can expect to see the impressive Sierra de Callosa mountain range, the fertile plains of the Vega Baja del Segura with its orchards, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1828095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also venture near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32106903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hondo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a significant wetland area known for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Callosa De Segura offer scenic viewpoints. The Sierra de Callosa, rising dramatically from the plains, provides breathtaking vistas. You might also encounter viewpoints overlooking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1828095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque backdrops for your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Callosa De Segura?
A: The road cycling experience in Callosa De Segura is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which combines flat, scenic sections through agricultural plains with challenging climbs in the Sierra de Callosa. The mild Mediterranean climate, especially in winter, is also a significant draw for quality altitude training.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Callosa De Segura?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes available around Callosa De Segura on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy rides on mostly paved surfaces to challenging ascents with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Callosa De Segura are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891892291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the outskirts of Alicante — Circular from Callosa de Segura&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Salinas Church – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Callosa de Segura&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Callosa De Segura?
A: Callosa De Segura is particularly ideal for road cycling during the cooler months, making it a popular winter training paradise. Its mild Mediterranean climate allows for quality cycling without extreme cold, attracting professional teams and cyclists escaping harsher northern European winters. Spring and autumn also offer pleasant conditions, while summer can be very hot.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Callosa De Segura?
A: For family-friendly cycling, the dedicated Greenways (Vías Verdes) like the "Ciclorruta Los Almarjales" and "Ciclorruta Los Azarbes" are excellent choices. These routes are predominantly paved with minimal elevation changes, making them accessible and safe for families. They traverse the fertile Vega Baja del Segura, offering a relaxed experience through orchards and palm trees.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Callosa De Segura offers historical and cultural sites. The ancient Callosa Castle, built in 961, dominates the horizon from the Segura Range. The town's old quarter features narrow cobbled streets with a medieval layout, and sites like the Sanctuary of San Roque and La Cueva Ahumada add cultural depth to your explorations, often visible from or accessible near cycling routes.

Q: What is the Sierra de Callosa and how does it impact road cycling?
A: The Sierra de Callosa is an impressive limestone mountain range that dramatically rises 578 meters from the surrounding plains. It is a central natural feature and significantly impacts road cycling by providing challenging ascents and descents for those seeking routes with considerable elevation gain. It also offers breathtaking views and is a hub for various outdoor activities. You can learn more about it on &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierra_de_Callosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.
